text.title: Rust project structure: compile-time gotchas and agent antipatterns
text.summary: Rules preventing the most common structural mistakes: stale crate versions, missing pub mod declarations, sqlx offline mode, and workspace migration pitfalls.

rule.statement: Pin all dependencies to exact minor versions in Cargo.toml. Commit Cargo.lock for binary crates (not for libraries). Agents must read the existing Cargo.toml for versions before adding new deps.
rule.rationale: Axum 0.6 and 0.7 have breaking router-state changes. An agent picking the "latest" version may generate code incompatible with the rest of the codebase.

antipattern.description: Agent adds axum = "0.7" when the project is on axum = "0.6".
antipattern.reason: Axum 0.7 changed the router State API. Mixed versions cause trait-bound errors that are impossible to diagnose without knowing the version delta.

rule.statement: After creating a new .rs file in src/dir/name.rs, always add pub mod name; to src/dir/mod.rs. Verify by grepping mod.rs before reporting the task complete.
rule.rationale: A missing pub mod line produces "unresolved module" errors that are non-obvious and confuse agents into adding duplicate files.

rule.statement: Use SQLX_OFFLINE=true and commit .sqlx/ query cache files when sqlx::query! macros are used. CI must not require a running database to compile.
rule.rationale: sqlx::query! macros connect to DATABASE_URL at compile time. Without offline mode, CI fails on every PR that lacks database connectivity.

antipattern.description: Using 'a lifetime parameters on service structs: UserService<'a>.
antipattern.reason: LLMs emit lifetime parameters to borrow-check &Database, but tokio::spawn requires 'static. Use Arc<Database> or owned Database (which wraps Arc<PgPool> internally).

antipattern.description: Mixing axum and actix-web imports in the same file after context overflow.
antipattern.reason: Axum and Actix handler signatures are incompatible. Pin the framework in the system prompt and add a CI check for the wrong import.

antipattern.description: Forgetting to update default-members and CI paths when promoting to a Cargo workspace.
antipattern.reason: cargo build --workspace must be proven to pass before the migration commit. Partial workspace migrations leave dead code that confuses subsequent agents.
